hints.text: Plates white w/ a noticeable **red block near the left** (sometimes an EU-style blue strip too)

shared_with.note: Serbia will almost always have a blue strip on their plates.
shared_with.diff: Red block near left → North Macedonia.

hints.text: All Gen4 = **smallcam**: low-mounted camera, big circular blur w/ small front protrusion (can be fully transparent)

shared_with.note: Most of the Balkans and southern Europe have smallcam.
shared_with.diff: Smallcam only narrows to the region.
Ѓ/Ќ Cyrillic → North Macedonia; Greek alphabet → Greece.

hints.text: The Google car **never has an antenna** — every other European country sometimes does in Gen3
hints.uniq_note: Only Serbia is also antenna-less MOST of the time — but Serbia does have an antenna sometimes. No antenna ≠ proof; an antenna DOES rule out North Macedonia.

shared_with.note: Serbia has no antenna the majority of the time, but sometimes does.

hints.text: Two bollard types: **thin rounded** + **wedge-shaped** — both white w/ red or white reflectors on a black surface

shared_with.note: Croatia uses the same wedge-shaped bollard.
shared_with.diff: Cyrillic → North Macedonia; č/ć Latin → Croatia.

shared_with.note: Similar wedge bollard but with more white space above the black rectangle.
shared_with.diff: More white above the rectangle → Hungary.

hints.text: Direction signs **yellow w/ biscriptal text** (Latin + Cyrillic town names)

shared_with.note: Serbia is the only other yellow-sign country with biscriptal text.
shared_with.diff: Ѓ/Ќ letters → North Macedonia; B-profile guardrails → Serbia.
Cyrillic on the sign → not Slovenia.
Cyrillic on the sign → not Croatia.

hints.text: **Macedonian Cyrillic**: **Ѓ** and **Ќ** are unique to Macedonian (Љ Њ Џ shared only w/ Serbian); road signs biscriptal, other signs fully Cyrillic
hints.uniq_note: Ѓ/Ќ = 100% Macedonian. Without those letters, Cyrillic alone could also be Serbian/Bulgarian.

hints.text: Landscape: relatively **dry grasslands**, small hills, scattered trees

shared_with.note: Found in Greece too (hence the name).

hints.text: **Yellow-filled warning signs** (most of Europe uses white)

shared_with.note: Greece also commonly uses yellow-filled warning signs.

hints.text: Gen3 coverage **sparse**: some highways + only **Skopje, Bitola, Kumanovo, Prilep, Tetovo, Veles, Ohrid, Strumica** have significant city coverage; large apartments/buildings → almost guaranteed **Skopje**

bullets: Kumanovo: the only ones w/ a **thick light-blue border**
Bitola: horizontal, light/dark blue, cross in-between the border (gap in rectangle)
Ohrid: horizontal, red or blue; cross UNDER the border (unique when red)
Strumica: vertical, blue, **intricate corner pattern** (only city)
Veles: vertical, blue, biggest variety; only city w/ cross ABOVE border when blue
Prilep: vertical, black; border doesn't go fully around
Tetovo: vertical, black; cross above border OR thick cross in-between

hints.text: **City spotlights**: black-yellow striped kerbs → Strumica (at roundabouts → Tetovo); **Ohrid** = only large city on a lake; long rectangular lamps → only Prilep; two extremely rocky hills → Prilep
Gen3 E-65 Kichevo–Arbinovo: a **green truck** behind the Google car (mostly obsolete)
